entity.name: Oluwajuwon Omotayo
entity.description: Independent open-source developer building AI governance infrastructure for African regulatory compliance. Creator of comply54 and agt-policies-nigeria — the only open-source runtime enforcement engine for NDPA 2023, CBN NIP Framework, NIMC Act 2026, NFIU, KDPA 2019, POPIA, and 15+ African regulations. Both projects are cited in the Microsoft Agent Governance Toolkit (AGT).

projects.name: comply54
projects.description: Open-source AI governance enforcement engine for African regulatory compliance. Intercepts AI agent tool calls and evaluates them against 21 African regulatory frameworks — CBN NIP Framework, NDPA 2023, NIMC Act 2026, NFIU MLPPA, KDPA 2019, POPIA, Uganda DPPA, Tanzania PDPA, Ethiopia PDP, and more — before execution. Blocked calls never reach the tool. Ships with production-ready adapters for LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen (v0.4), and OpenAI Agents SDK. No OPA binary required; works in serverless environments. Holds OpenSSF Best Practices Passing badge.

channels.description: Wire transfer or bank payment. Contact ginuxtechacademy@gmail.com for bank details and tax documentation.

plans.name: Annual Sustainability Grant
plans.description: Sustains full-time development of comply54 across four areas: (1) New African jurisdiction packs — Rwanda, Ghana, Egypt, Morocco, Senegal, Côte d'Ivoire; (2) Regulatory tracking — African data protection laws change frequently and policy packs must stay current; (3) Framework adapters — maintenance and new integrations as LangGraph, AutoGen, and CrewAI evolve; (4) Community and adoption — documentation, tutorials, and outreach to African fintech developers who need this infrastructure but don't yet know it exists.
